PhD Candidate · Civil & Environmental Engineering

Ebubechukwu
Al-Ihekwaba

Advancing sustainable pavement engineering through research in cold recycling technologies, mechanistic-empirical design frameworks, and machine-learning-driven material characterization.

Highly motivated individual focused on producing exceptional results and advancing quality through technical rigor, adaptability, and strategic problem-solving. I combine strong intellectual capability, versatility, and a solution-oriented approach to enhance performance and support organizational advancement.

Education & Experience

Aug 2022 – Present
University of New Hampshire
Research Assistant
  • Analyze pavement functional distress (IRI) and structural distress (rutting)
  • Execute FWD backcalculation and analyze deflection basin indices for structural capacity
  • Develop AASHTO 1993 empirical layer coefficients for recycled asphalt pavement materials
  • Build mechanistic-empirical rutting transfer function frameworks
  • Develop VBA-based Excel tool for material property prediction and quality assurance
  • Characterize binder rheology; evaluate resilient modulus, tensile & triaxial shear strength
  • Design and analyze national surveys on pavement recycling and reclamation practices
  • Disseminate research through peer-reviewed publications and international presentations
Feb 2022 – Jul 2022
BILAAD Realty, FCT-Abuja, Nigeria
Graduate Intern · Project Planning & Scheduling
  • Planned and scheduled infrastructure construction works using Microsoft Project
  • Prepared Planned vs. Actual reports and S-Curve templates for progress tracking
May 2021 – Feb 2022
Federal Ministry of Works & Housing, Nigeria
Graduate Engineer
  • Reviewed contract documents and progress reports for federal highway projects (South-South region)
  • Performed site inspections and post-construction evaluations; supported project close-out
Jan 2019 – Jun 2019
China Civil Engineering Construction Corp
Industrial Trainee · Lagos Airport Terminal Project
  • Performed plate bearing tests for apron pavement subgrade evaluation
  • Supervised reinforcement placement and concrete casting for key structural components
  • Executed QC and materials testing: concrete cubes, slump, reinforcement tensile, pile integrity
  • Compiled monthly progress reports for stakeholder coordination

02

Research Projects

NRRA · MnDOT

Cold Reclamation and Recycling Techniques to Achieve Perpetual Pavements

Investigating reclamation and recycling techniques to achieve perpetual pavement characteristics, focusing on long-term structural performance.

LRRB · MnDOT

Improving and Developing Pavement Design Inputs and Performance Functions for Cold Recycled Pavement Layers in Minnesota

Improving and developing pavement design inputs and mechanistic-empirical performance functions calibrated for cold recycled pavement layers in Minnesota conditions.

NCHRP Topic 55-04

Current Practices and Guidelines for Full-Depth Reclamation (FDR) — NCHRP Synthesis 657, Topic 55-04

National synthesis of guidelines and best practices for Full-Depth Reclamation (FDR) — published as NCHRP Synthesis 657.

NCHRP Topic 56-02

Current Practices and Guidelines for Design, Production, and Placement of High RAP Asphalt Mixtures — NCHRP Topic 56-02

Documenting national current practices and guidelines for high reclaimed asphalt pavement content mixtures.
